source.name: U.S. Department of State — Student Visa (F/M)

extraSources.name: Study in the States (DHS) — Financial ability

requirements.label: Form I-20 from a SEVP-certified school
requirements.detail: An I-20 issued by a SEVP-certified school after admission; it states your program's cost of attendance.

requirements.label: Proof of funds (per your I-20)
requirements.detail: Show you can cover the cost of attendance listed on your I-20 (tuition + living). There is no single fixed national figure — it is set by your school's I-20.

requirements.label: I-901 SEVIS fee
requirements.detail: Pay the I-901 SEVIS fee (USD 350 for F-1) before your visa interview.

requirements.label: DS-160 + visa interview
requirements.detail: Complete the DS-160, pay the visa application fee, and attend an interview at a U.S. embassy/consulate.

requirements.label: Health insurance
requirements.detail: U.S. healthcare is expensive — arrange student health insurance (some schools require their own plan).

requirements.label: Valid passport
requirements.detail: A passport valid at least 6 months beyond your stay.

requirements.label: Tuition / funds transfer
requirements.detail: Transfer tuition with a low-FX provider.

requirements.label: Connectivity on arrival
requirements.detail: An eSIM gives you a U.S. number and data on landing.

requirements.label: Accommodation
requirements.detail: Arrange on- or off-campus housing early.
